decision_rationale: A timestamp-safe liquidation-exhaustion event study is not immediately feasible in current qfa. OKX public API shape checks show timestamped BTC-USDT-SWAP liquidation prints plus trades and 1m candles are reachable, so the core event data is plausible. However, historical tradable depth/spread data required for stressed execution was not proven from public APIs, Binance derivative APIs are restricted from this host, Bybit is country-blocked, and current qfa has no crypto-perp liquidation/depth ingestion or event-study cost evaluator. Any performance claim now would fabricate missing depth, slippage, venue coverage, or same-bar timing assumptions.

fee_model.notes: Liquidation-exhaustion entries are assumed mostly taker/urgent until proven otherwise; fee model documented only because no event-study performance was run.

data_feasibility_findings.timestamp_safe_liquidation_prints: plausible_on_okx_public_api_for_recent_or_paginated_history; not proven cross-venue without vendor or authorized exchange access
data_feasibility_findings.tradable_price_history: plausible via OKX trades/history-candles and Binance data archives where accessible; must align contract identifiers and event timestamps
data_feasibility_findings.historical_depth_or_spread_history: not_proven_from_public_lightweight_checks; Binance public data archive has bookDepth files but Binance liquidation API was restricted and OKX public check only proved current book endpoint
minimum_needed_before_performance: Connector for timestamped liquidation prints with side, size/notional, venue, symbol, contract metadata, pagination bounds, and raw-to-normalized schema tests.
Same-venue BTC/ETH perpetual trades or 1m-or-finer tradable prices with conservative event availability lag and no same-bar lookahead.
Historical bid/ask spread and depth snapshots, or a documented conservative proxy calibrated from real order-book history, retained outside git.
Event labeling that uses only liquidation intensity observed at or before the decision timestamp, including deceleration/exhaustion and price-impact-stall features.
Cost/slippage evaluator with maker/taker/fill-mix, stressed spread/depth assumptions, clustered-event controls, and matched non-event/shifted-timestamp controls.

source_options_checked.evidence: HTTP 200 shape checks for /api/v5/public/liquidation-orders with state=filled on BTC-USDT SWAP returned details containing ts, time, side, posSide, sz and bankruptcy price; /market/history-trades, /market/history-candles?bar=1m and current /market/books were also reachable. Raw payloads were not retained.
source_options_checked.timestamp_safety_notes: Most promising no-credential path for liquidation prints plus prices. Still needs pagination-window proof, timestamp semantics, publication-lag policy, contract metadata, and historical depth/spread source before evaluation.

source_options_checked.source: Binance USD-M public API and Binance Data Vision
source_options_checked.evidence: fapi forceOrders, klines and depth API checks returned HTTP 451 restricted-location responses from this host. Binance Data Vision direct HEAD checks found BTCUSDT 1m kline and bookDepth daily ZIP files for 2024-01-01, but a same-pattern liquidationSnapshot file returned 404. No archives were downloaded or retained.
source_options_checked.timestamp_safety_notes: Useful only if authorized API access or a verified liquidation archive/vendor feed is available; cannot combine accessible price/depth archives with unavailable liquidation prints without a separate timestamp-safe event source.

source_options_checked.evidence: v5 market recent-trade and kline checks returned HTTP 403 CloudFront country-block responses.
source_options_checked.timestamp_safety_notes: Do not assume availability for this infrastructure; possible only from authorized location or licensed vendor data.

source_options_checked.evidence: Derivatives instruments endpoint returned HTTP 200, proving basic reachability, but historical liquidation/depth coverage was not proven in this scout.
source_options_checked.timestamp_safety_notes: Potential supplementary venue only after endpoint-level proof for forced-liquidation events and tradable depth/prices.

source_options_checked.evidence: Liquidation-history endpoint shape check returned application JSON with code 401 / API key missing, indicating credentials are required. No key was used and no vendor data was retained.
source_options_checked.timestamp_safety_notes: Plausible vendor route if licensed fields include exchange timestamp, side, size/notional, symbol mapping, and enough history.

source_options_checked.source: Kaiko, Amberdata, Coin Metrics or equivalent vendors
source_options_checked.evidence: No credentials, raw dumps, or caches were used. These vendors are candidates for normalized historical liquidations/trades/order-book data if licensed.
source_options_checked.timestamp_safety_notes: Best path for robust cross-venue event studies if point-in-time fields, retention policy, and redistribution limits are documented.
